author: nero_eth
content: Yeah the 75/25 exec/data split still holds (was already based on eip-7976) but ignores the BAL that will contribute to the block size via maxxing out SLOADs using the 75% of gas available.

author: nero_eth
content:
I created this dashboard allowing to play around with different configs:
https://nerolation.github.io/glamsterdam-worst-case-block-size/worst_case_block.html

But essentially, we can set a more-or-less aggressive payload deadline, e.g. second 6 (or less aggressive) 7, and, assuming an SLOAD cost of 3000 gas, would end up at ~4.5 mib worst-case block size.

author: nero_eth
content:
With an attestation deadline at 3s, and a payload deadline at 7s, we'd end up with 4s propagation time, much more than we have today.
Even a payload deadline at 6s would increase propagation time compared to today (although, ofc, we're now dealing with 2x the worst-case block size compared to today).

author: nero_eth
content:
Regular blocks are much smaller than those that a (malicious) builder could craft, ranging from 50-400kib (block incl BAL, snappy compressed, based on historical blocks today at 60M gas limit). 
Assuming a linear increase in size over increasing gas limits, we'd get to 250kib-2mib blocks at 200M gas limit. 
This seems easy doable having a 3-4s propagation window.
Avg. would be ~650kib.
